layer_1_flower.intent: Calm euphoria melting tension like warm sandalwood oil

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Lemongrass carries the dominant myrcene. Black Pepper matches the sandalwood aroma note. Pine provides α-Pinene support. Sweet Basil rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A low-energy session. Calm euphoria melting tension like warm sandalwood oil. Genres: ambient, dub, lo-fi hip hop, deep house. Tempo around 65-90 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Doctor Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Sandalwood and mint infused chamomile blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 185°F.
Add chamomile tea bag and steep for 5 minutes.
Add sandalwood-infused honey and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add fresh mint leaves and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Doctor Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz sandalwood-infused honey syrup and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz fresh mint leaves.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with sandalwood.

cocktail.name: Doctor Sour
method: Combine 2 oz bourbon, 1.5 oz sandalwood-infused honey syrup, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz fresh mint leaves.
Dry shake for 5 seconds (if using egg white).
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with sandalwood.

juice.name: Doctor Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh apple through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Doctor Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz bourbon with 0.75 oz fresh apple juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Truffle Popcorn with Cracked Pepper
snack.desc: Fresh-popped corn drizzled with truffle oil and fresh-cracked black pepper.

appetizer.name: Blistered Shishito Peppers with Miso Aioli
appetizer.desc: Flash-charred peppers served with white miso and yuzu aioli.

main.name: Duck Confit with Fig Glaze
main.desc: Slow-cooked duck leg with fig reduction, served over creamy polenta.

dessert.name: Fig & Honey Panna Cotta
dessert.desc: Vanilla bean panna cotta with roasted figs and a wildflower honey drizzle.

cheese.name: Brie & Fig Preserves
cheese.desc: Triple-cream Brie with fig preserves, honeycomb, and toasted hazelnuts.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your calm space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Low seating, soft throws, candles at eye level. Minimize overhead lighting.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 30 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the diffuser. Complete all cycles before consumption.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Close your eyes. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, letting the scent fill your lungs. Hold gently for four. Release slowly through the mouth for eight counts, feeling gravity pull you deeper into your seat with each exhale.

layer_13_narrative.text:
19 drops of lemongrass carry the dominant Myrcene through the room slowly — the way Greenshock Farms intended when they cultivated Doctor Greenshock in Willits, Mendocino County. The lineage reads Lavender X (Bubba Kush X Recon), but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: sandalwood, mint, and aged hash.

The second oil — Black Pepper — adds 9 drops of brightness that matches the mint note in the flower itself. Calming is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body settles into euphoric with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 1.66% total can deliver.

In the dimly lit room, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of Myrcene, already primed for calm euphoria melting tension like warm sandalwood oil. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Greenshock Farms grows in Willits, Mendocino County: not just cannabis, but context. The indica genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as rosemary in a Mendocino garden. The session settles into its final form — tension relieving, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at Mount Tamalpais State Park, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Petite Sirah's dense, jammy character mirrors myrcene's heavy, sedating quality — both deliver weight and body.
Grenache's lush fruit and herbaceous undertones complement myrcene's tropical warmth.

beer.terpene_bridge: Citra and Simcoe hops are myrcene-dominant — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins sharing the same terpene synthesis pathways.
beer.tasting_note: Tropical citrus, pine resin, clean bitter finish

beer.terpene_bridge: EKG hops carry significant myrcene, connecting malt-forward English ales to cannabis terpene profiles.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
